data: Do AI systems learn and leverage political opinions of users in recommendations? AIPM as a project is a point of articulation between algorithmic and social dynamics, and thus proposes a computer science and sociological angle to the question of the political impact of algorithms on social media. The project builds on ideological social network embedding methods and political surveys research to propose a double network and opinion space modeling of digital public spaces. Using this double network and spatial opinion analysis, the project proposes to test whether algorithms learn and leverage political opinions of users through algorithmic explainability, how they affect information dynamics in public debate, and to open a path towards actionable tools capable of guiding algorithm design, governance, and policy.
Pedro Ramaciotti Morales, Tim Faverjon, Antoine Vendeville
